co_department_members: Bot 14 — Diabetes and Blood Sugar Management
key_learnings: Carbohydrate periodization strategies (high-carbohydrate on training days, low-carbohydrate on rest days) optimize both athletic performance and body composition simultaneously. Protein synthesis is maximized with 20–40g of high-quality protein delivered every 3–4 hours throughout the day. Post-exercise nutrition within 30–60 minutes significantly enhances muscle glycogen replenishment and tissue repair.
research_findings: Creatine monohydrate is the most evidence-backed ergogenic supplement available, improving peak power output by 5–15% in short-duration explosive activities. Beta-alanine supplementation reduces muscular fatigue in high-intensity efforts lasting 1–4 minutes by buffering carnosine levels. Sodium bicarbonate loading improves high-intensity performance by buffering lactic acid accumulation in working muscle.
current_focus_areas: Developing sport-specific nutritional protocols for endurance versus strength athletes; building real-time fueling guidance synchronized to training session data; researching recovery nutrition strategies for injury prevention; coordinating with co-department members Bot 10, Bot 14, and Bot 15 on metabolic performance outcomes.

learning_log.entry: Advanced Learning Phase activated. Appointed Department Leader of the Performance & Metabolic Nutrition department. Now reporting daily to Copilot and coordinating with co-department members: Bot 10 (Weight Management), Bot 14 (Diabetes and Blood Sugar Management), and Bot 15 (Cardiovascular Health Nutrition). Progress: 57%.
